data.title: Palantir (PLTR) vs. Oracle (ORCL): AI Analyst Picks the Best AI Stock for 2026
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Oracle (ORCL) rank among the top global AI infrastructure providers.  TipRanks' AI Analyst rates PLTR "Outperform" but rates ORCL with a "Neutral" rating. It praises Palantir's U.S. growth and AI focus, while flagging Oracle's high leverage and negative cash flows.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Interestingly, the AI Analyst's ratings are quite contrasting to Wall Street's projections. On TipRanks, PLTR has a Hold consensus rating while ORCL has a Moderate Buy consensus rating.

TipRanks' AI Stock Analysis tool provides quick, data-driven stock evaluations to help investors quickly spot potential. It combines insights from top models like OpenAI's (PC:OPAIQ) GPT-4o and Google's (GOOGL) Gemini. The AI analysis also weighs both positive and negative factors influencing the company's stock performance.

Positive on Palantir's AI Potential

Palantir stock earns a solid score of 74 out of 100 on the AI tool based on the OpenAI 4o model. It also assigns a price target of $203, implying 4.7% upside from current levels.

Key positives include Palantir's robust U.S. revenue growth and product adoption that drive expanding U.S. market reach and are key to its long-term success. PLTR's Northslope partnership boosts its AI capabilities and market presence, strengthening its edge and innovation potential across industries. Also, its strong cash generation capability gives financial flexibility to invest in growth and handle economic uncertainties.

On the other hand, Palantir's stagnant European growth limits its global expansion and revenue potential. Plus, a potential reduction in contract revenue could hurt Palantir's profitability, while regulatory scrutiny of its government contracts could cause reputational risks and disrupt future deals, impacting long-term operations.

Cautious About Oracle's High Leverage

Oracle stock earns a Neutral score of 66 out of 100 on the AI tool based on the OpenAI 4o model. It also assigns a price target of $215, implying nearly 9% upside from current levels.

Among concerning factors, Oracle's high leverage signals financial risk, limiting its ability to invest in growth and handle downturns. Moreover, the software giant's negative free cash flow strains operations and growth. Its capital-intensive AI infrastructure model pressures Oracle's resources, which stands to hinder long-term investments and profitability.

Meanwhile, the AI Analyst remains positive about Oracle's strong cloud revenue growth potential, AI integrations that boost product capabilities, giving a competitive edge in the growing AI market. Also, the multi-cloud strategy expands Oracle's reach and flexibility, meeting diverse customer needs and compliance, strengthening its market position.

Wall Street Prefers ORCL Over PLTR

Below, we can see how both ORCL and PLTR perform on TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool. ORCL's Moderate Buy consensus with a huge 56.8% upside potential exceeds the AI Analyst's target. PLTR's Hold rating indicates 2.8% downside risk, contrasting the AI Analyst's upside view.

To conclude, for 2026, the AI-driven upside appears to hinge more on Palantir's U.S. growth and adoption, while Oracle's opportunity relies on cloud and multi-cloud expansion despite leverage concerns.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Trump Blames 'Eggheads' for Bringing the Economy Down
data.description:
President Trump celebrated gross domestic product (GDP) growing by 4.3% in the third quarter, although he noted that the stock market isn't responding accordingly due to fears of the Fed raising interest rates in response to inflation.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"A Nation can never be Economically GREAT if 'eggheads' are allowed to do everything within their power to destroy the upward slope," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Tuesday, adding he wants the new Fed Chair to lower rates if the market is performing well. Jerome Powell, the current Fed Chair, is set to be replaced by a Trump nominee once his term expires in May 2026.

Inflation Stays Above Target with Core PCE at 2.9%

Alongside the GDP update, the Bureau of Economic Analysis noted that core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) rose by 2.9% during the quarter. Core PCE is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation and remains above the central bank's target of 2%.

"Anybody that disagrees with me will never be the Fed Chairman!" Trump said.

The market now expects an 86.7% chance of the Fed holding rates steady next month, up from 80.1% a day ago, according to CME's FedWatch tool. The odds of a 25 bps rate cut fell to 13.3% from 19.9%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Gains as Board Urges Restraint From Investors
data.description:
The move to buy entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has brought with it a panoply of strange bedfellows and deep concern. Perspectives are wild and varied, and one of the strangest just emerged recently. Warner's own board is warning shareholders not to move on any offer from anybody, and in fact, hold their collective positions. This is proving satisfactory to shareholders, who sent Warner shares up over 1.5% in the closing minutes of Monday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Paramount Skydance (PSKY) has changed its offer substantially from the last time it changed its offer, and brought with it a lot of speculation. The hostile offer now has the backing of one of the planet's wealthiest men, and that is likely changing minds all over. The Warner board, perhaps sensing control slipping away, is therefore urging shareholders not to make any move at all until the latest offer can be reviewed.

The Warner board's response was surprisingly clear: "Warner Bros. Discovery stockholders are advised not to take any action at this time with respect to the amended Paramount Skydance tender offer." Gerry Cardinale with RedBird Capital noted, "…(the trust issue is a) red herring that is out there and preventing our offer from getting vetted and taken seriously, so we took it off the table. It is now off the table."

SAG-AFTRA Takes a Hand

What if they threw a sale, and all the employees left? That point is starting to raise its head up in the process, as the Screen Actors Guild – American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG-AFTRA) is looking to take a hand in the process. In fact, another strike is in the works, though it may not ever get to that point.

With actors increasingly concerned over the state of the industry—everything from residuals to actual jobs is in play here, along with decreased bargaining power—the actors are looking to step in themselves and halt the moviemaking process again just by no longer acting. But once more, Netflix (NFLX) is seen as the bigger threat here, as there is no sign that SAG-AFTRA will launch a strike if Paramount comes out on top instead.

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on seven Buys and 13 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 172.51% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $23.92 per share implies 17.98% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: New Raptor Models, Skills Training Programs No Help for Ford Stock (NYSE:F)
data.description:
The news out of legacy automaker Ford (F) really should have been better than it was. The news itself was quite good, featuring plans for new models of Ford Raptor coming out and new skills training programs to address the lack of mechanics in the United States. But investors could not get behind the news at all, and sent Ford shares down modestly in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The F-150 Raptor was a major move for Ford, with some believing that it fundamentally changed the landscape for the truck market. In fact, some even note that just about every other carmaker out there borrowed one point or another from the Raptor. And now, Ford is poised to change the game again by bringing out a new line of Raptors. Specifics are short on the ground right now, but some wonder if the Mustang will be next in line for a Raptor version.

Reports suggest that the Raptor has been applied to the F-150, the Bronco, and even the Ranger. But word from Ford's chief financial officer Sherry House suggests more will follow. House noted, "I know that some people in the audience here just had a wonderful ride in some of our Raptors, and that's a product that I think that we're going to be doing a bit more of as well. We're going to be leaning into these products that are just passion products. I mean, these are vehicles that people love."

Improving Skills

Ford has been right in the thick of things when it comes to sounding an alarm on blue collar skill in the United States. And it has also been front and center when it comes to addressing the shortfall. In fact, Ford just got together with SkillsUSA to improve opportunities to make mechanics out of high school students.

The new partnership calls on Ford and SkillsUSA to develop new manufacturing study programs, as well as automotive programs, for both high school and postsecondary students, particularly in the areas in and around Ford plants. Grants are also being prepared to cover costs and get the broadest numbers of students in the programs, and also, opportunities are being opened up to bring in the current generation of mechanics, or what is left of them, to serve as competition judges and the like.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on two Buys, 10 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 34.33% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.27 per share implies 0.34% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Cryptocurrency Thefts Hit a New Record in 2025
data.description:
Cybercriminals stole a record $2.7 billion in cryptocurrency in 2025, according to blockchain-monitoring firms such as Chainalysis, TRM Labs, and De.Fi. The year saw dozens of hacks targeting crypto exchanges and decentralized finance (DeFi) projects. The largest attack by far was a breach at Dubai-based exchange Bybit, where about $1.4 billion was stolen. Both blockchain analysts and the FBI said that the attack was carried out by North Korean government hackers, who have been the most active crypto thieves in recent years.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That Bybit breach is now considered the largest crypto theft ever, and one of the biggest financial heists in history. It easily surpassed previous record hacks, including the $624 million Ronin Network theft and the $611 million Poly Network breach in 2022. In addition to exchange hacks, Chainalysis also tracked another $700,000 stolen from individual crypto wallets. Altogether, the data shows that crypto theft has been steadily rising, from $2 billion in 2023 to $2.2 billion in 2024, before hitting a new high in 2025.

North Korean hackers were responsible for at least $2 billion of the stolen crypto in 2025, according to Chainalysis and Elliptic, and have stolen roughly $6 billion since 2017. Authorities say that these funds are used to support North Korea's sanctioned nuclear weapons program. Other major hacks last year included $223 million stolen from Cetus, $128 million from Balancer, and more than $73 million from Phemex.

Is Bitcoin a Good Buy?

Using TipRanks' technical analysis tool, the indicators seem to point to a negative outlook for Bitcoin. Indeed, the summary section pictured below shows that five indicators are Bullish, compared to six Neutral and 11 Bearish indicators.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Banks Such as JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Offer Spot Crypto Trading
data.description:
A growing number of U.S. banks, including JPMorgan Chase (JPM), are starting to offer spot cryptocurrency trading, homing in on the territory of brokerages such as Coinbase Global (COIN) and Robinhood Markets (HOOD).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Following a rule change by the U.S. federal banking watchdog, JPMorgan and other financial institutions are exploring crypto trading services for institutional investors. The move comes after the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) allowed national banks to engage in crypto trading.

Even before the OCC's rule change, several large U.S. banks had begun laying the groundwork for crypto trading. JPMorgan Chase has been one of the first banks out of the gate, having developed blockchain-based settlement infrastructure and offering crypto-linked products to institutional clients.

Other Participants in Crypto Trading

Other banks pushing into the spot trading of Bitcoin (BTC) and other cryptocurrencies include Goldman Sachs (GS), which has restarted its crypto trading desk. BNY Mellon (BK) has launched digital asset custody services for institutional clients, integrating crypto into its existing custody services.

Some banks have also partnered with cryptocurrency market makers and exchanges to provide execution, custody, or fiat rails, arrangements that could now expand into direct brokerage models. For leading crypto exchanges such as Coinbase and Robinhood, the entry of traditional banks into spot crypto trading presents them with a new competitive threat.

Is Bitcoin a Buy?

Most analysts don't offer ratings or price targets on Bitcoin. So instead, we'll look at the three-month performance of BTC. As one can see in the chart below, the price of Bitcoin has fallen 21.69% in the last 12 weeks.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: January Rate Cut Odds Slump on Resilient Economic Growth
data.description:
The Fed is set to meet on January 28 for its Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting, although the odds of a rate cut have fallen even lower after the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) announced that U.S. gross domestic product (GDP) grew by 4.3% during the third quarter, above the estimate of 3.3% and rising from 3.8% during the prior quarter.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, inflation remains persistent, with core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) tallying at 2.9% during the quarter. Core PCE is the Fed's preferred gauge on inflation and remains above the central bank's target of 2.0%.

January Rate Cut Odds Drop to 13.3%

The odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the January FOMC are now at 13.3%, down from 19.9% yesterday and 24.4% a week ago, according to CME's FedWatch tool. With the economy posting strong growth and inflation still above target, the Fed has more reason to hold rates steady. A lower rate can drive inflation higher, as it tends to stimulate consumer and corporate spending, and overheat the economy.

The Fed has a dual mandate of maximizing employment and keeping inflation at bay. While the labor market has shown signs of softening, several Fed officials believe that the risk of inflation outweighs concerns about slowing jobs growth.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slips Despite Power of Fab 52
data.description:
A new report emerged about chip stock Intel (INTC), and one of its primary processing operations: Fab 52. In fact, there are clear signs that Fab 52 puts one of Intel's primary competitors, TSMC (TSM) to shame in the United States. Despite what should have been welcome news, Intel shares still slipped fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The reports note that Fab 52 is actually superior to TSMC's United States operations on two critical fronts: Fab 52 is bigger, and it has better equipment. Not only is Fab 52 able to outmatch TSMC's Fab 21 phase one, it also outranks the Fab 21 phase two operations that are still on the forthcoming list. Fab 52 also has sufficient production capability to be a match for both of TSMC's fabs combined, reports note.

Fab 52 handles the 18A node, and can incorporate other technologies like gate-all-around RibbonFET transistors, the PowerVia backside power delivery network, a set of four ASML Twinscan NXE Low-NA EUV lithography systems, and more. In fact, Fab 52 is capable of producing around 40,000 wafer starts per month, which is the level that a "very large fab" can reach by current standards.

New Firmware Emerges

Only a couple weeks stand between us and the Consumer Electronics Show (CES), one of the biggest events in consumer-facing tech of any given year. Intel is getting ready for the big event, planning to show off new Panther Lake laptops armed with a set of new drivers. And Intel just finished getting one such driver ready: Linux support for the "50xx" neural processing unit (NPU) on Panther Lake.

The new driver represented a major step forward, as the firmware binary will be a vital part of getting the IVPU kernel driver up and running and starting up the NPU. And while only time will tell just how well the whole thing works, the early word suggests that this should work out nicely. If it works as expected, it should be a feather in Intel's cap at a time when it needs such feathers desperately.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on five Buys, 20 Holds and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 78.28% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $38.09 per share implies 5.13% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: IonQ Expands Global Footprint with 100-Qubit Quantum Deal in South Korea
data.description:
IonQ has entered a deal with the Korea Institute of Science and Technology Information (KISTI) to deliver a 100-qubit quantum system to South Korea. This marks another step in the company's efforts to expand global access to quantum computing.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The system is expected to be installed at KISTI's supercomputing center over the next few years, giving researchers, universities, and industry partners direct access to advanced quantum hardware.

For IonQ, the deal strengthens its presence in Asia and supports its goal to make quantum computing more easily available for real-world applications.

Also, the partnership will help accelerate national research in areas such as materials science, logistics optimization, cybersecurity, and next-generation AI.

By integrating IonQ's trapped‑ion quantum technology with its existing high-performance computing infrastructure, KISTI aims to build a hybrid environment where classical and quantum systems work together.

IonQ Boosts Swiss Presence

The deal comes just days after IonQ expanded its partnership with QuantumBasel, the quantum initiative of uptownBasel in Switzerland. The new agreement gives QuantumBasel ownership of its current IonQ Forte Enterprise system and a next-generation Tempo system, bringing the total value of the partnership to more than $60 million. It also extends IonQ's on-site presence in Switzerland through 2029.

IonQ CEO Niccolo de Masi said the extended partnership is a key part of the company's global strategy, helping build a quantum-ready ecosystem across Europe.

Is IonQ a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, IONQ has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and three Holds assigned in the last three months. At $75.91, the average IonQ price target implies 46.66% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: NASA Turns on NASA: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Slips as Safety Panel Takes NASA to Task
data.description:
Sometimes, the worst thing that can happen is when an organization's own safety panels take issue with its decisions. Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) found itself in a similar situation as a NASA safety panel took issue with NASA's handling of the Starliner incident. This did nothing particularly good for Boeing, as investors sent shares down fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks This Holiday Season

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The safety panel in question—the Aerospace Safety Advisory Panel (ASAP)—recently released a report that made NASA itself look terrible, pointing to "…a wave of uncertainty that lurked behind Boeing's unfortunate saga…." It also, reports noted, cast "…doubt over whether NASA can properly address safety concerns with future missions like Artemis."

ASAP, perhaps armed with the benefit of hindsight, asserted that NASA should have declared the entire incident a "mishap." Such a declaration would have immediately launched an investigation from the NASA safety office. Former shuttle commander and ASAP member Charlie Precourt noted, "The ASAP finding is the lack of a declared in-flight mishap or high visibility close call contributed to an extensive, excessive … period of time where risk ownership and the decision-making authority were unclear."

Improving Picture

We have noted, over the last few weeks, that Boeing is doing quite well right now, bringing in fresh contracts from the United States Navy and other branches of the military. This in turn is giving analysts a reason to pay more attention, as Moody's, just about two weeks ago, gave Boeing credit a solid rating as well.

The new contracts are contributing to better cash flow, and better cash flow makes debt repayment that much more likely. Moody's recently reaffirmed Boeing's Baa3 senior unsecured rating, and revised its outlook, now declaring Boeing "Stable" as improvements in quality, safety, and liquidity took hold.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 13 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 20.91% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $249.92 per share implies 15.38% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson
